response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's profile is fundamentally misaligned with the non-negotiable core requirements of this role. The position mandates a minimum of 8 years of C++ experience, yet the resume reflects approximately 6 months of C++ work at National Instruments and a single bachelor thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his self-reported skills list. His career is primarily in web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), with no demonstrated ownership of a cloud compute platform, no hardware or storage architecture experience, and no formal technical leadership of infrastructure teams. Distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby is a partial positive but insufficient to compensate for the depth of gaps across the minimum qualifications.
strengths: Hands-on distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency, async I/O requirements
Polyglot breadth across multiple paradigms and runtimes demonstrating genuine technical adaptability
Community-facing mentorship through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects indicating communication and knowledge-sharing capability
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — roughly 6 months at National Instruments versus the 8-year hard minimum; the skill is absent from his own resume skills section
No evidence of cloud compute platform leadership or ownership; cloud exposure is limited to AWS CDK for a pricing microservice and Kubernetes for a personal open-source side project
Career trajectory is application-layer and web-focused with no background in hardware architecture, storage systems, or compute infrastructure at any meaningful scale
